summary: Joel Spolsky provides a brief travel update from his World Tour promoting FogBugz. He reports on a 150-person turnout in Arlington, Virginia, and previews an upcoming live-streamed demo in Austin, Texas. He notes the absurd cost of hotel internet and mentions a minor travel mishap with Delta's automated check-in machines.
key_points: The FogBugz World Tour drew about 150 attendees in Arlington, Virginia
A live stream of the Austin demo was being arranged in partnership with NewTek, highlighting the logistical challenges of early streaming technology
Hotel internet pricing ($100 for a meeting room connection) was already a pain point for business travelers in 2007
money_quotes: To get a high-speed internet connection in the meeting room, we have to pay the hotel an extra $100. After all the other hotel bills, that actually sounds kind of reasonable, believe it or not.

After the demo we managed to break the automated check-in machines at the Delta airlines check-in counter, trying to move to an earlier flight. Eventually a nice human at the counter straightened it out.
key_insight: A lightweight travel diary from Joel's product tour, offering a snapshot of the logistical realities of software promotion and business travel in 2007.
